category.name: security-and-protection
category.preferred_workflow: Prefer simple documented Aspose.Words security workflows. Use Document.Protect, Document.Unprotect, LoadOptions, OoxmlSaveOptions, DocSaveOptions, PdfSaveOptions, and related documented APIs only when directly relevant to the task. Keep validation narrow and task-specific.

core_rules: Create local sample source documents when a task refers to an existing file, folder, stream, or template.
Do not assume external files or folders already exist.
Always save the requested protected or unprotected output document.
protection_rules: Use Document.Protect and Document.Unprotect for document protection workflows.
Use documented load and save options for password-protected workflows.
Do not invent protection, encryption, or permission APIs.
api_safety_rules: Do not invent unsupported Aspose.Words security or protection APIs.
Use documented protection, password, load, and save APIs only.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Generated code references members that do not exist in the selected package version.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Replace invented members with documented Aspose.Words APIs already proven in this category.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: The example assumes source files, folders, images, or data already exist.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Create deterministic local inputs before loading, processing, or validating them.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: The example fails at runtime while checking unrelated document internals.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Validate only the requested behavior and the existence of expected outputs.

examples.task: Create a CancellationTokenSource and pass its token to Document.Load to enable interruption.
Attach a callback to DocumentLoadingArgs.ThrowIfCancellationRequested during loading to ensure proper response.
Use token.ThrowIfCancellationRequested inside a field update loop to abort long-running operations efficiently.
Implement a low-code UI button that calls CancellationTokenSource.Cancel to stop processing immediately.
Combine a CancellationToken with Document.SaveAsync to allow asynchronous saving cancellation by checking token status.
Monitor token.IsCancellationRequested during layout building and exit the layout routine promptly if needed.
Handle OperationCanceledException after a cancelled Document.Load to perform necessary cleanup properly.
Log cancellation events with timestamps to an audit file for compliance tracking.
Pass the same CancellationToken to both loading and saving methods for consistent interruption control.
Use token.IsCancellationRequested in a custom LINQ Reporting Engine query to abort large reports.
Wrap a batch of Document.Load calls in a foreach loop that checks token cancellation before each load.
Configure DocumentLoadingArgs to invoke a user-defined method when cancellation is requested during loading.
Integrate CancellationToken into a background worker that processes documents without blocking the UI.
Validate that long-running field updates respect the token by inserting periodic cancellation checks.
Create a reusable helper method accepting a CancellationToken to perform safe document processing.
Ensure resource leaks are prevented by disposing the Document object after catching OperationCanceledException.
Use token.ThrowIfCancellationRequested inside a custom image extraction routine to stop early if needed.
Combine token monitoring with progress reporting to inform users when cancellation occurs during processing.
Implement a timeout mechanism that triggers CancellationTokenSource.Cancel after a predefined duration automatically.
Pass the token to DocumentBuilder operations to allow interruption while constructing complex documents.
Test cancellation behavior by simulating user aborts during document layout generation in unit tests.
Document the required .NET version for CancellationToken support in the project's README file.
Use token.IsCancellationRequested within a while loop processing document nodes to enable graceful exit.
Provide a configuration setting that enables or disables cancellation support for specific processing stages.
Capture and log the stack trace when OperationCanceledException is thrown for debugging purposes.
Apply token checks before invoking external resource retrieval to avoid unnecessary network calls.
Create an extension method that adds cancellation support to existing synchronous Document.Save calls.
Ensure that the CancellationTokenSource is disposed after processing completes to free system resources.
Demonstrate chaining multiple CancellationTokens using CancellationTokenSource.CreateLinkedTokenSource for complex workflows in applications.
Verify that document processing pipelines respect cancellation when integrated with third-party reporting tools.
